top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The US-Iran reset dominates: Trump says Tehran agreed to nuclear inspections 'into infinity' while Iran denies the details and bars the UN watchdog from its bombed sites, even as Iran says it will administer the Strait of Hormuz. A deadly heatwave grips Europe — around 40 people drowned in France and red alerts cover Spain and Italy. Markets wobbled as South Korea's KOSPI plunged ~10% in a tech-led Asian sell-off, while a Gaza ceasefire strain, a Montreal shooting and a Qatar gas-plant blast round out a heavy news day.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections 'into infinity'; Tehran denies
stories.summary: President Trump said Iran had agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back 'into infinity' following the Switzerland talks, but Iranian officials disputed the US account and said the IAEA would not be permitted to inspect sites bombed in the recent conflict. The US has suspended key Iranian oil sanctions as part of the emerging deal.

stories.headline: Iran says it will administer the Strait of Hormuz as Swiss talks conclude
stories.summary: Iran's chief negotiator said the Strait of Hormuz would be 'administered by Tehran' as the first round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland wrapped up with mediators reporting encouraging progress. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is preparing to travel to the Gulf to sell the reset to wary allies.

stories.headline: Israeli gunfire kills two in south Lebanon, testing Iran-linked ceasefire
stories.summary: Lebanese civil defence said Israeli fire killed two people in southern Lebanon, straining the Iran-linked ceasefire just as fresh Lebanon-Israel talks were set to begin in the shadow of the US-Iran deal. A UN survey estimated building damage in Lebanon's latest war at about US$1.38 billion.

stories.headline: Around 40 drown in France as record heatwave bakes Europe
stories.summary: French authorities reported around 40 drownings as people sought relief from a Europe-wide heatwave, with red heat alerts issued across France, Italy and Spain and temperatures forecast near 40C. Two children also died in a car in France amid the extreme heat.

stories.headline: South Korea's KOSPI tanks nearly 10% to lead a tech-fuelled Asia rout
stories.summary: South Korea's KOSPI plunged nearly 10%, dragged down by Samsung and SK Hynix, leading a broad sell-off across Asian markets. The yen hovered near a 40-year low against the dollar as investors digested the Wall Street decline and oil's slide on Iran peace-talk progress.

stories.headline: UN commission of inquiry says Israel is committing genocide in Gaza
stories.summary: A UN commission of inquiry concluded that Israel is committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children, in findings Israel has previously rejected as biased. The report adds to international pressure as Israeli strikes continue and ceasefire diplomacy proceeds.

stories.headline: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
stories.summary: Three people, including the suspect, were killed in a shooting in Montreal, Canada, with a police officer and a civilian among the dead. Investigators have not yet detailed a motive.

stories.headline: Qatar gas plant blast kills 13, injures dozens
stories.summary: An explosion at a Qatar gas plant killed at least 13 people and injured dozens, according to local authorities. The cause of the blast is under investigation.

stories.headline: Trump criticises Starmer as UK prepares for its seventh prime minister
stories.summary: President Trump criticised UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer over energy and immigration following Starmer's resignation announcement, as Britain prepares to install its seventh prime minister in a decade of political churn.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Cost-of-living and jobs lead the local day: civil servants get a 0.45-month mid-year bonus, core inflation held steady at 1.4% in May, petrol and diesel prices eased with oil, and Lazada cut about 5% of its Southeast Asia workforce including in Singapore. Expect afternoon thundery showers. In the buzz: a Punggol resident received a S$10 voucher as an apology for renovation noise, a man who exploited a PayPal glitch for free laptops turned himself in out of guilt, and durian prices are sliding.

stories.headline: Civil servants to get 0.45-month mid-year bonus
stories.summary: The Public Service Division said civil servants will receive a 0.45-month mid-year bonus, with junior-grade officers getting an additional one-time payment. The figure signals cautious optimism amid steady but soft economic growth.

stories.headline: Singapore core inflation holds steady at 1.4% in May
stories.summary: Core inflation stayed at 1.4% in May, MAS and MTI said, keeping price pressures subdued and within expectations as the economy navigates global uncertainty.

stories.headline: Lazada cuts about 5% of its Southeast Asia workforce, including Singapore
stories.summary: E-commerce firm Lazada is laying off about 5% of its workforce across Southeast Asia, including roles in Singapore. A union said it is supporting affected employees.

stories.headline: Petrol and diesel prices ease in Singapore as oil prices fall
stories.summary: Pump prices for petrol and diesel eased as global oil prices slipped on progress in US-Iran peace talks, offering modest relief to motorists.

stories.headline: Chan Chun Sing: rules on sea routes should be updated as risks grow
stories.summary: Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing said sea routes are more than avenues for trade and that international rules should be updated, warning that an erosion of major-power deterrence could spur adventurism and make the world more dangerous.

stories.headline: NLB sets timeline for Queenstown and Ang Mo Kio library revamps
stories.summary: The National Library Board unveiled its timeline for upgrades, with Ang Mo Kio Library set to move and Queenstown Library to close temporarily for a revamp.

stories.headline: Average number of rat burrows falls 40% in early 2026: NEA
stories.summary: The National Environment Agency said the average number of rat burrows uncovered fell about 40% in the first quarter of 2026, as authorities continued a cleanliness blitz against littering and waste.

stories.headline: Punggol resident gets S$10 FairPrice voucher from neighbour over renovation noise
stories.summary: A Punggol resident shared that a neighbour gave him a S$10 FairPrice voucher as an apology for renovation noise, a small gesture of neighbourliness that resonated online.

stories.headline: Man who got free laptops via PayPal glitch turns himself in out of guilt
stories.summary: A man who obtained free laptops by exploiting a PayPal glitch lodged a police report out of guilt and was given a detention order, an unusual case of self-reported wrongdoing.

stories.headline: Durian prices in Singapore drop, but sellers say they won't match Malaysia's lows
stories.summary: Durian prices in Singapore have fallen this season, though sellers say local prices are unlikely to match the lows seen across the Causeway in Malaysia.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran track is de-escalating on paper but disputed in practice: Washington suspended key oil sanctions and says inspectors will return, while Tehran denies the terms, bars the UN watchdog from bombed sites and claims it will administer the Strait of Hormuz. In Lebanon, fresh Israel talks open even as Israeli fire killed two, testing the ceasefire, and a UN inquiry found Israel is committing genocide in Gaza. In Ukraine, Russia is massing troops near a key Donbas city and rejecting the US as an 'objective mediator'.

theatres.name: US / Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz & nuclear talks)
theatres.status: De-escalation on paper but contested: the US waived key Iran oil sanctions and says inspectors will return, while Tehran disputes the terms, bars the UN watchdog from bombed sites, and claims sole control of the Strait of Hormuz.

timeline.event: First round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland ends with mediators reporting 'encouraging progress'.
US suspends key Iran oil sanctions; VP JD Vance calls it a 'good foundation' and says nuclear inspectors will return.
Iran's chief negotiator says the Strait of Hormuz will be 'administered by Tehran' as the Swiss talks conclude.
Iran says the UN nuclear watchdog will not be allowed to inspect sites bombed in the recent conflict.
Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections 'into infinity'; Tehran denies the US account. Rubio prepares to travel to the Gulf to sell the reset.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Russia masses troops near a key Donbas city and rejects the US as an 'objective mediator', while Ukrainian strikes squeeze Crimea's fuel supply and wrangling over the talks format drags on.

timeline.event: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets Russian oil facilities.
A Russian troop build-up threatens a city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas region.
Russia says the US is not an 'objective mediator' in Ukraine, complicating efforts to set a talks format.

theatres.name: Israel-Gaza / Lebanon
theatres.status: Fresh Lebanon-Israel talks open in the shadow of the US-Iran deal, but Israeli fire killed two in south Lebanon, testing the ceasefire; a UN inquiry found Israel is committing genocide in Gaza.

timeline.event: A UN survey estimates building damage in Lebanon's latest war with Israel at about US$1.38 billion.
New Lebanon-Israel talks are set to begin, in the shadow of the US-Iran deal.
A UN commission of inquiry says Israel is committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children; Israel has rejected such findings.
Israeli gunfire kills two people in south Lebanon, testing the Iran-linked ceasefire.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The DR Congo-Uganda Bundibugyo Ebola epidemic remains the most serious active outbreak: WHO's latest figures stand at 915 confirmed cases and 234 deaths, and WHO now says Congo's outbreak has recorded the highest first-month case total of any African Ebola outbreak. Kenya halted construction of a US-backed Ebola quarantine centre amid local opposition. The US measles outbreak continues to grind on at its highest level in decades.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) - DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Rapidly growing; WHO calls it the highest first-month case total of any African Ebola outbreak
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed cases only, DR Congo + Uganda combined (DRC 896 cases; Uganda 19 cases and 2 deaths), per WHO Disease Outbreak News DON608 (19 Jun 2026). At least 88 patients have recovered. More recent wire reports describe further growth not yet ratified in a formal DON.

outbreaks.name: Measles - United States
outbreaks.status: Largest US measles outbreak in decades; ongoing
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed US cases in 2026 per CDC, last reflected as of 18 Jun 2026; CDC's case tracker could not be re-fetched this run, so this figure is carried at its last confirmed value and date pending a fresh CDC update.

stories.headline: Congo's Ebola outbreak has most cases in first month of any African outbreak, WHO says
stories.summary: WHO said the DR Congo Bundibugyo virus outbreak has recorded the highest case total in its first month of any Ebola outbreak in Africa. The latest WHO Disease Outbreak News (19 Jun) counts 915 confirmed cases and 234 deaths across DR Congo and Uganda.

stories.headline: Kenya halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: A Kenyan minister said construction of a US-backed Ebola quarantine facility has been halted amid local opposition, as the region braces against spillover from the DR Congo-Uganda epidemic.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Supply-chain and social-engineering attacks lead this cycle: malicious npm packages posing as PostCSS tools drop a Windows RAT, WordPress's ShapedPlugin Pro range was backdoored, and a WhatsApp campaign uses fake business documents to install RMM software for remote access. On the vulnerability side, a 29-year-old Squid proxy bug ('Squidbleed') can leak cleartext HTTP, multi-tenant flaws hit the Dify AI platform, and FFmpeg and Microsoft AutoGen Studio both patched code-execution issues. India's Tata Electronics is also probing a breach that allegedly exposes Apple and Tesla trade secrets.

stories.headline: Malicious npm packages pose as PostCSS tools to deliver a Windows RAT
stories.summary: Researchers found malicious npm packages impersonating popular PostCSS tooling that install a Windows remote access trojan on developer machines, the latest in a run of open-source supply-chain attacks.

stories.headline: Tata Electronics probes breach allegedly exposing Apple, Tesla trade secrets
stories.summary: India's Tata Electronics is investigating a cyber breach in which attackers claim to have exposed trade secrets tied to clients including Apple and Tesla. The claims are not yet independently verified.

stories.headline: WhatsApp phishing campaign uses fake business documents to install RMM tool
stories.summary: A campaign abuses WhatsApp to deliver fake business documents that run VBScript and quietly install ManageEngine RMM software, giving attackers remote control of victim PCs.

stories.headline: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro plugins backdoored in supply-chain attack
stories.summary: Pro versions of ShapedPlugin's WordPress plugins were found backdoored in a supply-chain compromise, potentially giving attackers access to sites running the affected paid add-ons.

stories.headline: 29-year-old Squid proxy bug 'Squidbleed' can leak cleartext HTTP requests
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed 'Squidbleed', a long-standing flaw in the widely used Squid caching proxy that can leak cleartext HTTP request data. Admins are urged to update Squid deployments.

stories.headline: DifyTap flaws in the Dify AI platform could expose chats across tenants
stories.summary: Researchers detailed 'DifyTap' flaws in the Dify open-source LLM-app platform that could let one tenant access AI chats and data belonging to others in shared deployments.

stories.headline: OXLOADER loader spreads CastleStealer via malicious Google Ads
stories.summary: A new loader dubbed OXLOADER uses malicious Google Ads to deliver the CastleStealer infostealer, harvesting credentials and data from infected systems.

stories.headline: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in US$15 million crypto theft
stories.summary: The well-known JaredFromSubway maximal-extractable-value (MEV) trading bot was hacked, with attackers making off with about US$15 million in cryptocurrency.

stories.headline: Canada's spy agency used a first-of-its-kind warrant to clean botnet-infected devices
stories.summary: Canada's CSE obtained a first-of-its-kind warrant to remotely remediate botnet-infected devices, a notable expansion of active defence powers against compromised infrastructure.

stories.headline: FFmpeg patches 'PixelSmash' flaw in a widely used video decoder
stories.summary: FFmpeg fixed 'PixelSmash', a flaw in a widely used video decoder that could be triggered by malicious media files. The library is embedded in countless applications, broadening the exposure.

stories.headline: FortiBleed campaign used a custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
stories.summary: Attackers behind the FortiBleed campaign deployed a custom sniffer on compromised FortiGate devices to harvest credentials, following earlier reports that the flaw hit tens of thousands of appliances.

stories.headline: AryStinger botnet infects thousands of legacy routers to build a proxy network
stories.summary: The AryStinger botnet has infected thousands of legacy D-Link routers worldwide, conscripting them into a reconnaissance proxy network. Owners of end-of-life devices are urged to replace or isolate them.

stories.headline: Microsoft fixes AutoGen Studio flaw that enabled code execution
stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in AutoGen Studio, its agentic-AI development tool, that could allow code execution, underscoring growing security scrutiny of AI-agent tooling.
